# THRIVE Functional Templates

All examples are fictional and synthetic. A template is a working form, not approval, authority, publication or an official record.

## Thirty-template launcher, schema and persistence registry

Every template has one opening path, closed schema, partition, owner and persistence rule. If a template field has no declared schema path, persistence blocks; it is never dropped into generic prose or additional properties.

| Template | Launcher | Workflow/setup step | Closed schema | Partition | Owner | Persistence state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| TPL-01 | Learn, Govern & Grow | First-run private setup | `whole_life_private` | private owner partition | private owner | optional owner-controlled persistent |
| TPL-02 | Ground Truth & Offer | S0/S1 identity setup | `operator_authority_profile` | tenant governance domain | authority verifier | persistent until authority expiry |
| TPL-03 | Ground Truth & Offer | Source-watch setup | `source_claim` | tenant registry domain | source/applicability owner | persistent versioned registry record |
| TPL-04 | All launchers | Every CHART gate | `control_audit_receipt` | same tenant governance domain | named decision owner | tamper-evident receipt only |
| TPL-05 | Ground Truth & Offer | WF-01 | `mission_goal` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-06 | Ground Truth & Offer | WF-02 | `source_claim` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-07 | Ground Truth & Offer | WF-03 | `content_asset` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-08 | Ground Truth & Offer | WF-04 | `privacy_consent_intake` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-09 | Reach & Relationships | WF-10 | `audience_aggregate` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-10 | Ground Truth & Offer | WF-05 | `journey_touchpoint` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-11 | Ground Truth & Offer | WF-06 | `content_asset` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-12 | Reach & Relationships | WF-07 | `content_asset` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-13 | Reach & Relationships | WF-08 | `experience_theme` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-14 | Reach & Relationships | WF-09 | `relationship_partner` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-15 | Reach & Relationships | WF-11 | `relationship_partner` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-16 | Reach & Relationships | WF-12 | `event_outreach_plan` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-17 | Run Services & Campaigns | WF-13 | `campaign_plan` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-18 | Run Services & Campaigns | WF-14 | `content_asset` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-19 | Run Services & Campaigns | WF-15 | `channel_experiment` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-20 | Run Services & Campaigns | WF-16 | `operations_handoff` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-21 | Run Services & Campaigns | WF-17 | `service_offering` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-22 | Run Services & Campaigns | WF-18 | `financial_scenario` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-23 | Learn, Govern & Grow | WF-19 | `financial_scenario` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-24 | Learn, Govern & Grow | WF-20 | `financial_scenario` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-25 | Run Services & Campaigns | PWR-16 manager supplemental | `operations_handoff` | tenant operations domain | wellness manager | persistent aggregate/process draft |
| TPL-26 | Learn, Govern & Grow | WF-22 | `privacy_consent_intake` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-27 | Learn, Govern & Grow | WF-21 | `source_claim` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-28 | Learn, Govern & Grow | WF-23 | `agent_charter_trace` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-29 | Learn, Govern & Grow | WF-24 | `control_audit_receipt` | tenant domain declared by schema | workflow owner | persistent draft only after schema/gate; otherwise discard |
| TPL-30 | Learn, Govern & Grow | Phase 5 guided adoption | `mission_goal` | tenant planning domain | operator | persistent bounded plan; no activation state |

## TPL-01 — Personal Mission, Career & Protected-Life Compass

**Required fields:**

- [ ] personally controlled store verified
- [ ] private mission
- [ ] career aspiration
- [ ] values
- [ ] protected-life promise
- [ ] Minimum Mode trigger
- [ ] 90-day goal
- [ ] review/delete date

**Worked synthetic example:** Fictional manager Maya protects Friday evening for family and chooses one 90-day leadership experiment.<br>
**Validation:** owner confirms isolation and can revise/delete.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** never available to employer analytics or cross-lane memory.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-02 — Role, Organization, Service & Delegated-Authority Card

**Required fields:**

- [ ] exact role/title
- [ ] active hat
- [ ] organization/site
- [ ] approved service scope
- [ ] authority source
- [ ] decision rights
- [ ] exclusions
- [ ] escalation route
- [ ] expiry

**Worked synthetic example:** Maya records 'Wellness Services Marketing Manager' and $0 purchasing authority in the synthetic setup.<br>
**Validation:** source/verifier/expiry required.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** title, credential and authority stay separate.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-03 — Source, Claim & Freshness Ledger

**Required fields:**

- [ ] publisher
- [ ] exact title
- [ ] canonical URL
- [ ] date/version/status
- [ ] exact claim
- [ ] population/limits
- [ ] conflicts
- [ ] reviewer
- [ ] expiry

**Worked synthetic example:** A fictional benefit statement remains Proposed because its source population does not match the intended audience.<br>
**Validation:** claim must link to current source or block.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** not a legal or clinical determination.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-04 — CHART Authority & Action Gate Receipt

**Required fields:**

- [ ] C context/consequence
- [ ] H human authority/hat
- [ ] A authorization/access/action
- [ ] R rules/risk/resources/records
- [ ] T trace/test/transition/termination
- [ ] gate result
- [ ] owner decision
- [ ] timestamp
- [ ] reconciliation

**Worked synthetic example:** A synthetic email launch blocks because exact copy and release owner are missing.<br>
**Validation:** all five dimensions pass for consequential use.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** receipt cannot grant authority.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-05 — Mission, Wellness Promise & Ethical Growth Compass

**Required fields:**

- [ ] approved mission
- [ ] service scope
- [ ] truthful promise
- [ ] non-goals
- [ ] audience aggregate
- [ ] three outcomes
- [ ] burden measures
- [ ] 90-day focus
- [ ] owner/review

**Worked synthetic example:** Fictional Harbor Wellness chooses 'make services easier to understand' and explicitly avoids outcome guarantees.<br>
**Validation:** named owner approves direction.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no fabricated commitment or clinical promise.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-06 — Claim Evidence & Safer-Language Card

**Required fields:**

- [ ] exact claim
- [ ] claim class
- [ ] source refs
- [ ] population
- [ ] limitations
- [ ] conflicts
- [ ] safer options
- [ ] reviewers
- [ ] approval/expiry

**Worked synthetic example:** 'Supports a calmer routine' is held for evidence and reviewer context rather than asserted as a health outcome.<br>
**Validation:** exact approved wording/context/version required.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no diagnosis, advice or guaranteed result.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-07 — Brand Trust, Promise-Gap & Issue Brief

**Required fields:**

- [ ] verified facts
- [ ] allegations
- [ ] unknowns
- [ ] approved promise
- [ ] experience evidence
- [ ] stakeholders
- [ ] response options
- [ ] correction route
- [ ] decision owner

**Worked synthetic example:** A synthetic delay issue separates three verified process facts from two unverified online statements.<br>
**Validation:** authorized owner approves response.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no public reply or review manipulation.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-08 — Consent, Disclosure, Testimonial & AI Transparency Map

**Required fields:**

- [ ] experience step
- [ ] data purpose
- [ ] choice/default
- [ ] consent scope/expiry
- [ ] material connection
- [ ] testimonial rights/provenance
- [ ] AI role
- [ ] withdrawal
- [ ] reviewers

**Worked synthetic example:** A fictional influencer concept blocks because material-connection wording and usage expiry are absent.<br>
**Validation:** rights, disclosure and withdrawal verified.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no testimonial use or consent capture.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-09 — Aggregate Audience Insight & Ethical Segment Card

**Required fields:**

- [ ] approved source
- [ ] purpose
- [ ] denominator
- [ ] segment definition
- [ ] minimum cell
- [ ] missingness
- [ ] stigma/exclusion check
- [ ] universal alternative
- [ ] expiry

**Worked synthetic example:** A synthetic segment uses broad 'prefers weekend programs' aggregate data and rejects health-condition inference.<br>
**Validation:** privacy/equity owner validates.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** never an individual lead/contact profile.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-10 — Human-Centered Service Journey & Friction Map

**Required fields:**

- [ ] approved service
- [ ] aggregate persona
- [ ] journey stages
- [ ] questions/emotions
- [ ] touchpoints
- [ ] promise/actual
- [ ] friction hypotheses
- [ ] handoffs
- [ ] measures/owner

**Worked synthetic example:** A fictional journey finds confusing pre-visit instructions and proposes an accessible content test.<br>
**Validation:** service owner validates process.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no booking, triage or individual record.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-11 — Accessibility, Language & Cultural Respect Review

**Required fields:**

- [ ] task/audience
- [ ] formats
- [ ] WCAG target
- [ ] keyboard/screen-reader
- [ ] plain language
- [ ] translation QA
- [ ] cultural assumptions
- [ ] low-tech option
- [ ] remediation owner

**Worked synthetic example:** A synthetic landing page needs keyboard focus repair and qualified Spanish review before release.<br>
**Validation:** qualified human verifies remediation.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** not an accessibility certification.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-12 — Human-Voice & Anti-AI-Slop Content Brief

**Required fields:**

- [ ] reader question
- [ ] purpose/action
- [ ] verified facts
- [ ] claim annotations
- [ ] source/owner/date
- [ ] uncertainty
- [ ] banned filler/clichés
- [ ] read-aloud notes
- [ ] author approval

**Worked synthetic example:** A draft replaces 'unlock your journey' with a specific date, service description and named contact route.<br>
**Validation:** human author owns final language.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no fabricated story, quote or intimacy.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-13 — Aggregate Theme & Feedback-Back Brief

**Required fields:**

- [ ] purpose/visibility
- [ ] synthetic or externally prepared aggregate/theme export ref
- [ ] human approval/time
- [ ] suppression/minimum cell
- [ ] complaint/adverse-event screen
- [ ] themes/interpretations
- [ ] uncertainty/dissent
- [ ] commitments
- [ ] feedback/delete date

**Worked synthetic example:** A fictional human-approved aggregate export reports two themes and one dissenting view without quotes or raw comments.<br>
**Validation:** experience/privacy owner validates export and prohibited-content screen.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no raw feedback, source phrase, individual sentiment, complaint narrative or contact list.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-14 — Community Relationship, Reciprocity & Commitment Ledger

**Required fields:**

- [ ] community-defined need
- [ ] representatives
- [ ] shared purpose
- [ ] power/burden
- [ ] resources/compensation question
- [ ] commitments/non-goals
- [ ] feedback-back
- [ ] owner
- [ ] exit/review

**Worked synthetic example:** A synthetic partner plan budgets interpreter access as an open human decision and promises feedback-back.<br>
**Validation:** both sides approve commitments.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no contact harvesting or executed promise.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-15 — Partner, Referral-Network & Co-Marketing Due-Diligence Matrix

**Required fields:**

- [ ] partner identity/authority
- [ ] mission/service fit
- [ ] roles
- [ ] clinical/referral boundary
- [ ] claims/evidence
- [ ] data flow
- [ ] economics/conflicts
- [ ] risk/exit
- [ ] reviewers

**Worked synthetic example:** A fictional gym partnership stays Under Review because referral neutrality and data sharing are unresolved.<br>
**Validation:** formal multidisciplinary review required.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no referral, contract or endorsement.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-16 — Event, Outreach & Community Activation Plan

**Required fields:**

- [ ] objective/non-goals
- [ ] aggregate audience
- [ ] venue/channel
- [ ] roles/capacity
- [ ] approved content
- [ ] access/language
- [ ] consent/safety
- [ ] contingency/stop
- [ ] measures/after-action

**Worked synthetic example:** A synthetic fair includes sensory-friendly hours and a paper information option; no attendee list is stored.<br>
**Validation:** event and safety owners approve.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no invite, registration, purchase or publication.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-17 — ALIGN Campaign Strategy & Release Canvas

**Required fields:**

- [ ] A aim/affected people
- [ ] L leadership/accountability
- [ ] I inputs/interdependencies
- [ ] G governance/gates
- [ ] N next state/named receiver
- [ ] status quo/non-AI option
- [ ] channels/capacity
- [ ] measures/stop
- [ ] human decision

**Worked synthetic example:** A fictional campaign compares updated service-page content with a no-campaign process improvement.<br>
**Validation:** all release owners sign exact version.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** canvas never launches or spends.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-18 — Editorial Calendar, Content Review & Retirement Board

**Required fields:**

- [ ] asset/version
- [ ] brief/owner
- [ ] channel/date
- [ ] claim/source links
- [ ] rights
- [ ] review sequence
- [ ] state
- [ ] expiry
- [ ] replacement/retirement

**Worked synthetic example:** A synthetic article is marked Expired when its cited guidance changes and enters replacement review.<br>
**Validation:** official approver/state required.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no publish, send or deletion.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-19 — Channel Experiment & Pre-Analysis Card

**Required fields:**

- [ ] decision/hypothesis
- [ ] baseline
- [ ] variants
- [ ] period/sample assumptions
- [ ] outcome/balancing measures
- [ ] exclusions
- [ ] guardrails/stop
- [ ] analysis
- [ ] human decision

**Worked synthetic example:** A synthetic two-headline test uses aggregate page completion and does not deploy tracking.<br>
**Validation:** pre-registration and owner interpretation required.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no launch, pixel or automatic winner.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-20 — Demand-to-Service, Team Capacity, SOP & Handoff Brief

**Required fields:**

- [ ] service/period
- [ ] aggregate demand/workload/capacity definitions
- [ ] numerator/denominator/freshness
- [ ] role/RACI
- [ ] retain/delegate/consult/escalate
- [ ] approved SOP refs/questions
- [ ] aggregate review queue and continuity
- [ ] handoffs/thresholds/options
- [ ] owners/reconciliation

**Worked synthetic example:** A fictional scenario models a 10–20% inquiry range, caps the aggregate review queue, proposes backup reviewer roles and routes all decisions to named managers.<br>
**Validation:** marketing/team/operations/service/HR/clinical owners validate their domains.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no individual lead or employee data, monitoring, scoring, scheduling, assignment, booking or routing.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-21 — Wellness Service Portfolio & Value-Proposition Map

**Required fields:**

- [ ] service facts
- [ ] scope/boundary
- [ ] aggregate job-to-be-done
- [ ] feature/experience/process/outcome
- [ ] evidence
- [ ] alternatives
- [ ] access/capacity
- [ ] value language
- [ ] approval

**Worked synthetic example:** A synthetic program says 'weekly guided sessions' as a service fact and leaves health benefit wording unapproved.<br>
**Validation:** service/clinical/legal owners approve.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no new service, efficacy claim or offer.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-22 — Budget, Marketing-Mix & Value-Learning Scenario

**Required fields:**

- [ ] budget version/period/currency
- [ ] sources
- [ ] actual/plan/forecast labels
- [ ] channel assumptions
- [ ] formulas
- [ ] base/upside/downside
- [ ] capacity/equity/burden
- [ ] value limits
- [ ] finance decision

**Worked synthetic example:** A fictional $10,000 planning envelope shows ±20% reach uncertainty and no guaranteed conversion or ROI.<br>
**Validation:** finance validates sources and math.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** not a transaction or accounting record.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-27 — Source, Policy, Platform Rule & Freshness Crosswalk

**Required fields:**

- [ ] use case/jurisdiction/channel
- [ ] publisher/title/URL
- [ ] date/version/status
- [ ] requirement/claim
- [ ] local policy
- [ ] applicability owner
- [ ] conflict/unknown
- [ ] control/evidence
- [ ] review/expiry

**Worked synthetic example:** A fictional platform rule is labeled Seed—Verify Current and cannot release a campaign.<br>
**Validation:** qualified owner resolves applicability.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no legal conclusion or compliance guarantee.<br>

## TPL-28 — ORBIT Agent Charter, Permission & Human-Handoff Envelope

**Required fields:**

- [ ] O objective/owner/non-goals
- [ ] R risk/rights/prohibited decisions
- [ ] B data/source/tool/destination/time/cost/expiry boundaries
- [ ] I independent fixtures
- [ ] T transfer/terminate/kill/rollback/purge
- [ ] PERM-P0 install state
- [ ] proposed one-run permission
- [ ] fallback/incident
- [ ] human approval

**Worked synthetic example:** A synthetic source checker permits public URLs in a sandbox only and remains PERM-P0 after install.<br>
**Validation:** independent human approves one run.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no self-activation or external action.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-29 — Agent Test, Run, Human Decision, Incident & Retirement Receipt

**Required fields:**

- [ ] agent/charter/model/prompt/policy/schema hashes
- [ ] run authorization/permission/expiry
- [ ] criterion + fixture payload/hash
- [ ] adapter/version/hash
- [ ] environment fingerprint
- [ ] expected/observed state/action/data deltas
- [ ] raw-log hash/reviewer/previous-receipt hash
- [ ] incident/kill/rollback/purge
- [ ] retirement

**Worked synthetic example:** A fictional draft agent fails a citation test; the tamper-evident receipt binds the exact fixture, adapter, environment, kill and purge evidence and release is denied.<br>
**Validation:** tamper-evident human decision receipt required.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** no hidden retry or self-attestation.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.

## TPL-30 — Seven-Day Launch & Ninety-Day THRIVE Growth Plan

**Required fields:**

- [ ] days 1-7 previews
- [ ] power states
- [ ] agents PERM-P0
- [ ] benefit/burden/failures
- [ ] day-7 retain/revise/pause/remove
- [ ] days 8-30 max-three powers
- [ ] days 31-90 one track
- [ ] material change/expiry
- [ ] uninstall test

**Worked synthetic example:** Maya previews Claim Check and Content Lab, activates neither, and schedules a day-7 decision.<br>
**Validation:** each milestone has evidence receipt.<br>
**Human gate:** a named, authorized human records accept, revise, defer, reject or delete with date/version.<br>
**Boundary:** zero active powers remains acceptable.<br>
**Safe finish:** save as draft in the allowed partition, route for human review or discard; no external action.
